free_text: Cross sections of 3He-particle induced reactions on
The stack consisted of six thin (13-25 mg/cm2)
tungsten targets deposited on 30 and 40 mu-m thick
aluminum supports. Tungsten targets were prepared
tungsten-186. The tungsten content is at least 99.86%,
(Al, As, B, Be, Ca, Co, Cr, Cu, Fe, Ge, Mg, Mn, Mo,
Na, Ni, Sc, Si, Ti, Zn) content is less than 0.01%.
The targets were produced by sedimentation.
ORTEC GEM 35P4 series HPGe detector with an
energy resolution of 850 eV for the 122 keV line and
1.8 keV for the 1330 keV line was used. The dead time
thick) were placed between the targets.
Irradiation was carried out until the total
charge on the target reached 0.1 mu-A h at an average
The results were compared to the data from the
The uncertainties of the energy were calculated using
the SRIM software based on the spread in the initial
beam energy of + - 1 MeV and the spread in the
thicknesses of degraders and supports (+ - 1 mu-m).
Entry was approved by Dr. A.N.Moiseeva
